name: Mucin-containing minimal medium + 10 mM L-lactate (PMC10786315)
description: Mucin-containing minimal medium + 10 mM L-lactate — medium reported in PMC10786315 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC10786315 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Full recipe recovered from the primary literature: base medium 'MCLMAN minimal medium (Campylobacter jejuni NCTC 11168 defined minimal medium) supplemented with 0.5% (w/v) partially purified gastric mucin' (Alazzam B, Bonnassie-Rouxin S, Dufour V, Ermel G. MCLMAN, a new minimal medium for Campylobacter jejuni NCTC 11168. Res Microbiol 162:173-179 (2011). DOI 10.1016/j.resmic.2010.09.024; PMID 21144899. As applied (0.5% gastric mucin + carbon source) by the DiRita lab: Sinha R, LeVeque RM, Bowlin
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.verification_evidence: SI Appendix: 'strains were grown in MCLMAN minimal medium containing 0.5% partially purified gastric mucin and various carbon sources, including sodium pyruvate, sodium butyrate and L-lactate (1)'; main text: 'We tested C. jejuni growth in mucin-containing minimal media supplemented with either 10 mM butyrate or 10 mM L-lactate'
provenance.recipe_evidence: SI Appendix (Materials and Methods): "To determine the growth of C. jejuni, strains were grown in MCLMAN minimal medium containing 0.5% partially purified gastric mucin and various carbon sources, including sodium pyruvate, sodium butyrate and L-lactate (1), as noted." Main text/SI: growth assays used L-lactate at 10 mM ("grown in minimal media with L-lactate (10mM)"). Culture atmosphere: "routinely grown under microaerobic conditions (85% N2, 10% CO2, 5% O2)." MCLMAN composition per Alazzam et al. 2011 (abstract): the medium "contains inorganic salts, the amino acids l-cysteine, l-leucine, l-
provenance.base_medium: MCLMAN minimal medium (Alazzam et al. 2011, Campylobacter jejuni NCTC 11168 defined minimal medium) supplemented with 0.5% (w/v) partially purified gastric mucin; carbon source 10 mM sodium L-lactate; microaerobic gas phase 85% N2 / 10% CO2 / 5% O2.
provenance.base_medium_reference: Alazzam B, Bonnassie-Rouxin S, Dufour V, Ermel G. MCLMAN, a new minimal medium for Campylobacter jejuni NCTC 11168. Res Microbiol 162:173-179 (2011). DOI 10.1016/j.resmic.2010.09.024; PMID 21144899. As applied (0.5% gastric mucin + carbon source) by the DiRita lab: Sinha R, LeVeque RM, Bowlin MQ, Gray MJ, DiRita VJ. Phosphate Transporter PstSCAB of Campylobacter jejuni Is a Critical Determinant of Lactate-Dependent Growth and Colonization in Chickens. J Bacteriol 202:e00716-19 (2020). DOI 10.1128/jb.00716-19; PMID 31932316; PMC7167465 (this is reference (1) cited in the PMC10786315 SI Appendix).

sources_chased.what: This paper's SI Appendix (Methods: Bacterial strains and media; Growth analysis in MH broth and minimal media)
sources_chased.how: curl NCBI OA supplementaryFiles zip (europepmc REST), extracted pnas.2316540120.sapp.pdf, pdftotext

sources_chased.what: MCLMAN base recipe (Alazzam B et al., Res Microbiol 162:173-179, 2011; DOI 10.1016/j.resmic.2010.09.024; PMID 21144899)
sources_chased.how: Attempted ScienceDirect, Elsevier, ResearchGate, academia.edu, EuropePMC, Semantic Scholar (openAccessPdf CLOSED), Unpaywall (oa_status closed), and 3 derivative papers (PMC7318876 M1cam GEM, PMC8531295 C. hepaticus, PMC7167465 Sinha 2020) — all paywalled or non-reproducing
added: confirmed MCLMAN component classes: 4 amino acids (Cys/Leu/Met/Asp), niacinamide, inorganic salts, lactate — but individual salt/iron/trace identities and ALL concentrations remain unresolved

sources_chased.what: Sinha et al. 2020 (PstSCAB, PMC7167465) = ref (1) of the SI carbon-source method
added: confirmed MCLMAN + carbon-source growth protocol and microaerobic gas mix; no mucin recipe here (mucin is novel to PMC10786315)

missing_before: MCLMAN inorganic salts + iron source + trace elements (still unresolved - closed-access Alazzam 2011)
recuration.notes: Grown microaerophilically (85% N2 / 10% CO2 / 5% O2), not fully aerobic; 'oxygen' kept per the model's O2/CO2 exchanges. The defined base is MCLMAN (Alazzam 2011): confirmed to contain 4 amino acids (L-Cys, L-Leu, L-Met, L-Asp), niacinamide, inorganic phosphate, unspecified inorganic salts + lactate. The complete salt/iron/trace table and every concentration exist only in the closed-access Alazzam 2011 Res Microbiol paper, which could not be obtained despite exhausting all open routes; those are listed by class and left unexpanded rather than fabricated.
